system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

{

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|omega|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|omega|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    50;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        U               0.7;
        p               0.3;
    }
}

writeCompression off;

runTimeModifiable true;')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Current Error Analysis

The error persists from Attempt 3. The `writeControl` keyword is set to `writeInterval` but the error still says "step is not in enumeration". This means the `writeInterval` keyword is also not recognized. Looking at the error message more carefully:

The valid options are: `adjustableRunTime`, `clockTime`, `cpuTime`, `runTime`, `timeStep`.

The keyword `writeInterval` is NOT in the list. The old-style `writeControl` with `writeInterval` as the value was replaced by a simpler enum-based system where the allowed values are those five listed.

In newer OpenFOAM versions (Foundation version), `writeControl` is a simple string that takes one of the five enumerated values directly â€” not a sub-dictionary with `writeInterval` inside it.

## Root Cause

The `controlDict` has `writeControl writeInterval;` but `writeInterval` is not one of the valid enum values. The user requires outputting every 0.01 units of time. The correct approach in modern OpenFOAM is to use `writeControl adjustableRunTime;` combined with setting `adjustableRunTime yes;` and then controlling the actual interval via the `writeInterval` directive outside the `writeControl` scope.

## Recommended Fix

**In `system/controlDict`:**

Change the `writeControl` entry from `writeInterval` to `adjustableRunTime`:

```
writeControl    adjustableRunTime;
```

Then ensure the rest of the file supports adjustable runtime writing. The full `writeControl` section should look like:

```
writeControl    adjustableRunTime;
writeInterval   0.01;
purgeWrite      0;
writeFormat     ascii;
writePrecision  6;
writeCompression off;
```

This tells OpenFOAM to use adaptive time stepping logic where it writes at intervals of 0.01 seconds, regardless of whether `adjustTimeStep` is active. The `adjustableRunTime` mode works with both `adjustTimeStep yes/no` and will respect the `writeInterval` setting.

## Summary

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `system/controlDict` | Change `writeControl step;` â†’ `writeControl adjustableRunTime;` |

This is the standard approach for modern OpenFOAM versions where `writeControl` accepts one of the five named enums directly, and `adjustableRunTime` is the appropriate choice for interval-based writing.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/controlDict', 'changes': 'Change writeControl from writeInterval to adjustableRunTime'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 10m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-5.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
